title: PivotalResearch Fellowship 2026 in London: Fully Funded AI Safety Fellowship with £8,000 Stipend (Apply by May 3)
summary: Applications are now open for the Pivotal Research Fellowship 2026 Q3 , an exciting fully funded opportunity for individuals passionate about ensuring the safe development of artificial intelligence. Hosted at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) , this prestigious fellowship offers participants the chance to spend 9 weeks in London , conducting high-impact AI safety research with expert mentorship, financial support, and the possibility of up to 6-month extensions . This fellowship is designed for aspiring and early-career researchers, policy thinkers, and professionals who want to contribute meaningfully to the future of AI governance, safety, evaluations, and control. About the Pivotal Research Fellowship Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming one of the most transformative technologies of our time. The Pivotal Research Fellowship exists to support talented individuals committed to ensuring AI develops safely and responsibly. The program provides fellows with access to leading researchers, structured research management, a collaborative in-person workspace, and financial support to help them focus fully on impactful research. Since its launch, the fellowship has successfully completed 7 cohorts , supported 129 alumni , and maintains an impressive 9.1/10 peer recommendation score . Notably, 70–90% of fellows who apply for extensions receive continued support. Fellowship Location and Dates The 2026 Q3 fellowship will take place: Dates: June 29 – August 28, 2026 Location: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), London, United Kingdom Selected fellows will work in-person at LISA’s collaborative workspace, with weekday lunch and dinner provided. Financial Benefits The fellowship is fully funded and offers substantial support, including: £6,000 – £8,000 stipend Travel expenses to London covered £2,000 housing support for fellows coming from outside London Meals included during weekdays Access to required research compute resources Possibility of up to 6-month paid extensions This financial package ensures fellows can fully dedicate themselves to meaningful AI safety research without financial stress. What Fellows Receive Expert Mentorship Participants receive weekly one-on-one mentorship sessions with established researchers who provide: Methodological guidance Critical feedback Strategic career direction Research supervision Dedicated Research Management Pivotal’s research managers help fellows: Scope and refine projects Stay on track with deadlines Navigate research challenges Plan long-term career growth Workshops and Networking Fellows also benefit from: Q A sessions with leading AI safety experts Hands-on workshops Exposure to policymakers and researchers Expanded professional networks Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is open to: Anyone committed to ensuring AI develops safely There are no strict degree or nationality restrictions mentioned. Applicants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds are encouraged to apply, especially those interested in: AI safety AI governance AI policy Evaluations AI agents Corporate governance AI control AI security AI for biology (AIxBio) Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation and intellectual curiosity in the field. Mentors You Could Work With Participants are matched with leading researchers and experts, including: Jide Alaga — Research Scientist, Google DeepMind Cozmin Ududec — Head of Science of Evaluation, UK AISI Samira Nedungadi Jasper Götting — SecureBio Adam Kaufman — Redwood Research These mentors provide direct guidance throughout the fellowship. Why You Should Apply If you want to build a serious career in AI safety, policy, governance, or technical alignment research, this fellowship offers one of the strongest early-career opportunities available globally. With expert mentorship, full financial support, elite networking opportunities, and pathways into top institutions, the Pivotal Research Fellowship can significantly accelerate your impact and career trajectory. Application Process The selection process includes four stages: Step 1: Written Application Applicants submit: Personal background Research interests Mentor preferences Estimated time: 1–2 hours Step 2: Video Interview A short recorded interview. Estimated time: Less than 10 minutes Step 3: Work Task A mentor-specific research exercise. Estimated time: 1–3 hours Step 4: Personal Interview Interview with the Pivotal team or mentors. Estimated time: 30–45 minutes Application Timeline Important Dates Application Deadline: May 3, 2026 Decisions Released: May 22, 2026 Fellowship Period: June 29 – August 28, 2026 Extension Period: Up to 6 additional months Career Outcomes for Fellows Past fellows have gone on to work with top institutions including: Google DeepMind Anthropic Fellowship UK AISI GovAI SaferAI AI Futures Project Institute for Progress Cooperative AI Foundation FLI Beneficial AI Foundation Others have: Founded projects such as PRISM Evals, Catalyze Impact, and Moirai Started PhDs at Oxford, Stanford, EPFL, and Max Planck Institute Published research in top academic journals and policy forums Referral Bonus Opportunity Know someone who would be a strong fit? You can recommend a candidate and receive $1,000 if they are accepted into the fellowship. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application before the deadline. Apply Here Apply for 2026 Q3 Fellowship You can also sign up to receive notifications for future fellowship cohorts. The future of AI will be shaped by the people working on it today. The Pivotal Research Fellowship 2026 Q3 offers a rare opportunity to join that effort, work alongside world-class mentors, and contribute to one of the most important challenges of our generation. If you are serious about building a career in AI safety and governance, this is an opportunity you should not miss. Deadline: May 3, 2026 Discover similar fellowship opportunities Here. title: BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026: Paid Global Development Internship for Students and Graduates
summary: Are you looking for a meaningful internship opportunity in international development? The BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026 offers a structured, paid pathway for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s largest development organisations. This six-month programme is designed to connect academic learning with real-world development practice while preparing the next generation of global leaders. About BRAC BRAC is an international development organisation founded in Bangladesh in 1972. It works with over 100 million people worldwide to address inequality and poverty, focusing on areas such as education, health, livelihoods, climate change, and social innovation. The BRIDGE Internship Programme is part of BRAC’s long-term commitment to developing young professionals who can contribute to solving global development challenges. BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme Overview The BRIDGE Internship Programme is a six-month, paid internship targeted at final-year students and recent graduates. It provides an opportunity to work on real development projects while gaining exposure to both field and office environments. During the programme, interns will: Work on projects across different regions in Bangladesh Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams Engage with real-world social and economic challenges Learn from experienced professionals and mentors Contribute to impactful development initiatives Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Bachelor’s degree from a recognised university or currently in the final semester Completion of all coursework (for students awaiting internship) Open to all academic disciplines Demonstrated interest in development work Participation in extracurricular activities is an added advantage Key Responsibilities Selected interns will: Support day-to-day operations of assigned projects Collaborate with departments, programmes, and field offices Prepare reports, presentations, and research outputs Contribute ideas to address development challenges Assist in organising meetings, events, and field activities Develop analytical and research-oriented problem-solving skills Internship Benefits Participants in the BRAC internship programme will receive: Monthly stipend of BDT 8,000 Certificate and Letter of Appreciation upon completion Mentorship from experienced professionals Exposure to large-scale development programmes Opportunities to work in both office and field settings Access to BRAC University library and research resources Subsidised meals and transportation support (conditions apply) Potential pathway to full-time employment at BRAC Why Apply for a BRAC Internship Key reasons to consider this opportunity include: Work with one of the world’s largest development organisations Gain practical, hands-on experience in international development Build professional skills relevant to global careers Receive mentorship and career guidance Increase chances of securing long-term roles within BRAC Internship Areas and Departments Interns may be placed across a wide range of programmes, including: Advocacy for Social Change Climate Change Communications Education Health Human Resources Microfinance Migration Technology Social Innovation Lab Youth Platform Global Partnerships and Thought Leadership Types of BRAC Internship Programmes BRIDGE Internship Programme (6 Months) Designed for final-year students and recent graduates Focus on in-depth learning and professional development Includes mentorship and potential transition to full-time roles Regular Internship Programme (3 Months) Open to undergraduate and postgraduate students Provides short-term exposure to BRAC’s programmes Focus on skill development and practical experience Application Process To apply for a BRAC internship: Review available internship opportunities Complete the official application form Prepare and submit: Updated CV (including passport-size photo) Letter of intention Email application to: internship@brac.net Clearly indicate the programme or department in the subject line Applications are assessed based on academic qualifications and relevant experience. Successful candidates are typically notified within two weeks after the application deadline. Application Deadline 10 May 2026 Apply Now BRIDGE Internship Programme Application Form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJxm9FpxH-Oso4rsnzq9OA76APRmZ5RKRk2PL_rc-M-DAY4Q/viewform https://www.brac.net/get-involved/internship/bridge-internship/ Internship Programme: https://www.brac.net/regular-internship Frequently Asked Questions Where are BRAC internships based? Internships are currently offered in Bangladesh. Are international applicants eligible? Yes. BRAC accepts international applicants, and English is widely used as a working language. Is the internship paid? Yes. Interns receive a monthly stipend. Is knowledge of Bangla required? No. Translator support is available if needed. Is accommodation provided? Accommodation is not provided, but guidance is available for international interns. What is expected of interns? Interns are expected to contribute to research, reporting, fieldwork, and presentations within their assigned departments. Conclusion The BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026 provides a strong foundation for anyone seeking a career in international development. With structured learning, mentorship, and real-world exposure, it offers a valuable opportunity to build skills while contributing to meaningful global impact. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare strong application materials that demonstrate their interest in development work and readiness to contribute. title: Just Tech Fellowship 2027 by Social Science Research Council (SSRC): $60,000 Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Creatives, and Practitioners in Technology & Society
summary: The Just Tech Fellowship 2027 , offered by the Social Science Research Council (SSRC), is a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed for individuals working at the intersection of technology and society . With an unrestricted award of up to $60,000 , this fellowship empowers researchers, artists, technologists, and practitioners to explore how technology shapes public life and to develop innovative, impactful solutions. This one-year fellowship program is not just about funding—it is a transformative platform that supports interdisciplinary collaboration, public engagement, and forward-thinking research that addresses some of the most pressing societal challenges driven by technological change. What is the Just Tech Fellowship? The Just Tech Fellowship is a flagship initiative of the Social Science Research Council aimed at supporting community-grounded, original, and socially impactful work that critically examines the role of technology in shaping society. The fellowship welcomes applicants from diverse backgrounds, including: Social scientists Humanists Artists Journalists Technologists Community-based researchers Public-interest practitioners The program emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration , ensuring that fellows contribute to a broader understanding of how digital systems influence governance, equity, public discourse, and everyday life. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support: Financial Award Up to $60,000 unrestricted funding to support: Research projects Creative work Community-based initiatives Professional Development Monthly virtual cohort gatherings Individualized mentorship support Access to the Just Tech network Collaboration Opportunities One in-person workshop for fellows Opportunities to collaborate across cohorts Eligibility for additional seed funding for joint projects Long-Term Engagement Continued access to the Just Tech network beyond the fellowship year Fellowship Duration Fellowship Period: January 2027 – December 2027 The program spans one full year, allowing fellows to deeply engage with their projects while participating in structured activities and collaborative exchanges. Fellowship Objectives The Just Tech Fellowship aims to: Advance Critical Research Support projects that interrogate how technology affects society Promote Public Engagement Encourage fellows to share their work with broader audiences Foster Interdisciplinary Dialogue Build bridges across fields and practices Support Innovative Thinking Enable creative and experimental approaches to technology-related challenges Areas of Focus Applicants are encouraged to propose projects that engage with: Digital technologies and public life Technology governance and accountability Artificial intelligence and ethics Data justice and privacy Social media and public discourse Community-centered tech innovation Technology and inequality Projects may involve: Research Creative production Policy engagement Community collaboration Application Requirements Applicants must submit a comprehensive application including: 1. Résumé/CV Maximum of 2 pages 2. Personal Statement Up to 1,000 words OR Video statement (maximum 5 minutes) 3. Work Proposal Written document (max 3,000 words) OR Project deck (up to 10 slides) The proposal must include: Concept description Engagement with technology Approach and contribution Feasibility plan Field context Public contribution 4. Work Samples Two samples showcasing previous work Brief descriptions explaining your role 5. AI Use Disclosure Applicants may use AI tools for assistance, but submissions must reflect original ideas and voice Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Residency Open to citizens of any country Must reside in the United States during the fellowship period SSRC does not sponsor visas Education No formal degree requirements Applicants may come from academic or non-academic backgrounds Availability Must commit to the full fellowship year Restrictions Full-time students are not eligible Experience Demonstrated engagement with issues at the intersection of technology and society Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Articulated Practice Clear and coherent body of work Strength of Project Relevance and originality Execution Capacity Ability to complete the project Public Engagement Evidence of sharing work with audiences Program Fit Alignment with fellowship goals Collaboration Willingness to engage with peers and contribute to the cohort Application Timeline Application Opens: April 27, 2026 Application Deadline: June 28, 2026 (11:59 p.m. EST) Fellows Notified: November 2026 Fellowship Period: January – December 2027 Why You Should Apply The Just Tech Fellowship stands out because it: Provides substantial funding ($60,000) Supports interdisciplinary innovation Encourages real-world impact and public engagement Connects fellows to a global network of experts Offers flexibility and creative freedom Click HERE to learn more and to apply Contact Information For inquiries, applicants can contact: just-tech@ssrc.org Discover more global Fellowship and Tech opportunities on OFY. title: One Million Coders Ghana: Transforming Africa’s Digital Future Through World-Class Tech Training
summary: In an era defined by rapid technological advancement, digital skills are no longer optional—they are essential. The One Million Coders (OMC) Ghana initiative stands at the forefront of this transformation, offering a groundbreaking opportunity for individuals to gain cutting-edge tech expertise and become part of a thriving digital ecosystem. Spearheaded under the leadership of John Dramani Mahama , this ambitious national initiative is designed to empower one million Ghanaians with globally competitive digital skills, positioning Ghana as a leading technology hub in Africa. What is One Million Coders Ghana? The One Million Coders Ghana program is a large-scale digital capacity-building initiative aimed at equipping citizens with industry-relevant skills in technology and innovation. It combines: High-quality training programs Expert-led mentorship Industry partnerships Access to modern learning infrastructure The goal is clear: build a future-ready workforce capable of driving economic growth through technology. Why One Million Coders Matters Digital transformation is reshaping industries across the globe. Ghana is taking proactive steps to ensure its citizens are not left behind. Key Objectives of the Program Empower individuals with digital skills that meet global standards Promote innovation and entrepreneurship in the tech ecosystem Enhance employability in both local and international markets Strengthen Ghana’s digital economy By participating, learners gain not just technical knowledge but also access to a vibrant community of innovators. Available Courses and Learning Paths The program offers a wide range of courses tailored to different career paths and skill levels. 1. Artificial Intelligence (AI) AI Essentials Duration: 10 hours Level: Beginner Learn to automate workflows, summarize data, and evaluate AI outputs AI Professional Duration: 10 hours Level: Beginner Integrate AI into real-world workflows and analyze complex data 2. Cloud Computing AWS Cloud Practitioner Duration: 70 hours Level: Beginner Learn cloud fundamentals, deployment, and security best practices 3. Software Development Back-end Development (PHP) Duration: 80 hours Level: Intermediate Focus on APIs, databases, and deployment Back-end Development (Python) Duration: 80 hours Level: Intermediate Build scalable backend systems C++ Essentials Advanced Duration: 38–70 hours Levels: Intermediate to Advanced Develop high-performance and system-level software 4. Data Protection Certified Data Protection Officer (CDPO) Duration: 40 hours Mode: In-person Level: Intermediate What You’ll Learn: Monitoring compliance with data protection laws Managing data risks and breaches Responding to incidents Ensuring regulatory compliance 5. Additional Learning Areas Cybersecurity Data Analytics Digital Marketing UI/UX Design Networking IT Support Project Management Digital Literacy Program Benefits Joining the One Million Coders initiative comes with significant advantages: 1. Access to World-Class Training Courses are designed to meet global standards and industry demands. 2. Industry Partnerships Collaborations with leading organizations such as: Google MTN Telecel Government institutions 3. Sponsored Learning Opportunities Eligible participants may receive fully or partially sponsored training . 4. Practical, Career-Focused Skills Programs are structured to prepare learners for real-world job roles. 5. Community and Networking Gain access to a network of: Tech professionals Mentors Fellow learners Learning Format and Accessibility Online and in-person options available Training centers distributed across regions in Ghana Flexible schedules to accommodate learners The initiative ensures global quality education with local accessibility , making it inclusive and scalable. How to Register Getting started is simple: Visit the official One Million Coders platform Browse available programs Select your preferred course Complete the registration process Once enrolled, you can immediately begin your journey toward becoming a tech professional. REGISTER HERE The Vision: Building Ghana’s Tech Future The One Million Coders initiative is more than a training program—it is a national movement . By empowering individuals with digital skills, Ghana is: Creating job opportunities Encouraging innovation Attracting global investment Strengthening its position as a tech leader in Africa Participants are not just learners—they are future leaders shaping the digital economy . Deadline No specific deadline has been stated. However, early registration is strongly encouraged as spaces in certain programs may be limited. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global training Opportunities on OFY here . title: GiveWell Researcher Job Opportunity 2026: Remote Global Role Paying Up to $162,400 to Drive High-Impact Global Change
summary: If you are passionate about using evidence, research, and analytical thinking to improve lives worldwide, the GiveWell Researcher opportunity could be one of the most impactful remote jobs available in 2026. GiveWell is hiring exceptional Researchers to help direct hundreds of millions of dollars annually toward the world’s most cost-effective global health and poverty reduction programs. This fully remote international role offers competitive compensation of up to $162,400 annually, outstanding benefits, global flexibility, and the chance to influence life-saving funding decisions at scale. About GiveWell GiveWell is a globally respected research organization focused on finding and funding the most effective opportunities in global health and development. Since 2007, GiveWell has directed more than $2.6 billion to high-impact programs and interventions, helping save over 340,000 lives. The organization is trusted by tens of thousands of donors who rely on GiveWell’s transparent and evidence-based recommendations. Why This Opportunity Stands Out Unlike traditional research roles, this position directly influences where major funding flows. Your analysis can shape decisions that affect millions of people worldwide. As a Researcher, you will evaluate interventions, analyze evidence, build cost-effectiveness models, consult experts, and recommend where GiveWell should invest resources for the greatest measurable impact. Salary and Compensation GiveWell offers highly competitive location-based compensation: $162,400 – New York City / San Francisco Bay Area $147,300 – Other U.S. locations International compensation aligned to comparable rates and local currency Location Fully Remote Open to U.S. and International Applicants Optional office locations in Oakland, Brooklyn, and London Relocation support available in select cases Visa sponsorship may be available for U.S.-based hires Key Responsibilities Successful applicants will work on some of the world’s most important development questions, including: Reviewing evidence on global health and poverty interventions Building cost-effectiveness models Evaluating grant applications and funding requests Advising on high-impact funding decisions Consulting academics, policymakers, and field experts Publishing transparent reports and findings Solving complex research questions with creative methods Example Areas of Research Researchers may analyze programs such as: Vaccine demand generation Vitamin A supplementation Seasonal malaria chemoprevention Water quality interventions Cash transfers Rural infrastructure projects Nutrition and livelihoods programs Career Growth Opportunities This role offers strong advancement pathways into: Senior Researcher Program Officer Senior Program Officer Team leadership and specialized grantmaking roles Professionals can grow into owning entire funding portfolios or leading strategic research agendas. Benefits Employees receive a strong benefits package, including: 100% funded health, dental, vision, and life insurance (U.S.) Four weeks paid time off 16 weeks fully paid parental leave Retirement plan Ergonomic home office support Coworking memberships Flexible working hours International travel opportunities Team retreats and conferences Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for candidates who: Care deeply about global health and poverty reduction Love rigorous research and evidence analysis Think critically and independently Communicate clearly Enjoy solving difficult problems Can prioritize high-value work Thrive in remote, collaborative environments Application Process The hiring process includes: Application review Short application exercise Two compensated work trials Several short interviews Applicants only need to submit a resume/CV and answers to application questions. No cover letter is required. Application Deadline There is currently no fixed deadline . Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early application is strongly recommended. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply directly through GiveWell’s careers portal and prepare a strong CV highlighting research, analytical, quantitative, and impact-focused experience. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The GiveWell Researcher position is more than a job—it is an opportunity to use your expertise to improve lives globally. With excellent pay, remote flexibility, and the chance to shape hundreds of millions in funding decisions, this is one of the most meaningful international research opportunities available right now. If you are ready to combine career growth with measurable global impact, this is the role to pursue. title: AHSS Global Fellows 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for International Academics
summary: International academics seeking funded research opportunities in the UK can now apply for the prestigious AHSS Global Fellows Scheme 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast. This highly competitive fellowship supports scholars from around the world to spend between 4 and 12 weeks in Belfast , collaborating with leading researchers and advancing shared academic interests. Hosted by the Faculty of Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences (AHSS) , the program offers an exceptional opportunity to build global research networks, access world-class university resources, and contribute to impactful academic discussions. What Is the AHSS Global Fellows Scheme? The AHSS Global Fellows Scheme is designed to bring outstanding international scholars to Queen’s University Belfast for short-term academic visits. Fellows will work closely with colleagues in one of the Faculty’s four Schools while pursuing collaborative research projects and knowledge exchange activities. This fellowship is open to academics at any career stage and across disciplines connected to the Faculty’s academic areas. Fellowship Benefits Successful applicants will receive a strong package of support, including: Funding for visits lasting 4 to 12 weeks Travel and accommodation funding up to £4,000 A courtesy desk within the host School Access to university library resources Opportunities to collaborate with respected academic staff Platform to deliver a lecture, seminar, or workshop International networking and research visibility Longer stays may be possible through self-funded arrangements. Available Host Schools Applicants may be hosted within one of the Faculty’s four Schools: Arts, English and Languages History, Anthropology, Philosophy and Politics Law Social Sciences, Education and Social Work Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: University lecturers Postdoctoral researchers Professors Independent scholars with institutional affiliations Early-career and senior academics seeking international collaboration Application Requirements Before submitting an expression of interest, applicants must first secure sponsorship from a host School within the Faculty. After selection, successful fellows will also need to complete the University’s formal Visiting Titles process. Deadline The deadline for applications for visits in the Autumn Semester of the 2026/27 academic year is: Friday, 26 June 2026 How to Apply Applicants should complete the official Expression of Interest form after receiving approval from a host School. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Why Apply to Queen’s University Belfast? Queen’s University Belfast is globally recognized for research excellence and interdisciplinary collaboration. Through this fellowship, scholars gain access to a vibrant academic community in one of the UK’s most historic and innovative cities. If you are looking to expand your international research profile, develop partnerships, and experience academic life in Belfast, the AHSS Global Fellows Scheme 2026/27 is an outstanding opportunity. title: Turn Your Idea into a Scalable Business with the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 | Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Social Entrepreneurs
summary: If you are building a business idea that can transform communities while driving the fair energy transition, the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 is your chance to grow faster, smarter, and with global support. This fully funded, five-month online incubation program is designed for early-stage entrepreneurs worldwide who want to create scalable and sustainable businesses in clean energy, environmental sustainability, and inclusive innovation. Delivered by Bridge for Billions in partnership with EDP , the program offers mentorship, training, networking, and investment readiness support at no cost. Whether you only have an idea, a minimum viable product (MVP), or an existing product still searching for a sustainable model, this program can help you validate your concept and build a clear growth roadmap. Why Apply for the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026? The global transition to fair and accessible energy requires bold entrepreneurs with practical solutions. This program is built to help innovators turn their ideas into real businesses that can scale impact. Participants will gain access to: 1. Personalized Expert Mentorship Work directly with industry professionals who will guide your progress, challenge your assumptions, and help refine your business strategy. Weekly mentorship sessions ensure accountability and continuous improvement. 2. Proven Business Incubation Methodology Use a structured, step-by-step framework to validate your business model, identify customer needs, and create a scalable strategy. 3. Investment Readiness Training Learn how to build financial forecasts, strengthen your go-to-market plan, and position your venture for future investors and partners. 4. Social Impact Measurement Define measurable outcomes, create KPIs, and prove how your venture benefits communities and systems over time. 5. Global Community Access Join a network of entrepreneurs, mentors, and professionals from around the world who are solving pressing social and environmental challenges. What Will You Learn? During the five-month incubation journey, selected founders will develop critical entrepreneurial skills, including: Building a unique value proposition Attracting and retaining customers Pricing products and calculating costs Marketing and communication strategies Financial planning for the next three years Measuring social impact Scaling operations and expanding reach Pitching your business effectively Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for founders whose projects focus on at least one of the following areas: Access to clean energy Energy efficiency Green job creation Re-skilling and up-skilling workers Environmental preservation Affordable and resilient solutions for underserved communities Applicants can come from any country and any background. No age or educational restrictions apply. Program Benefits Selected participants will receive: Free participation in a world-class incubation program Weekly mentorship from experts Access to 8 business development modules Networking sessions and live workshops Training in investment readiness and pitching International visibility for your startup Opportunity to pitch during the final Demo Day in November 2026 Important Dates Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 Selection Announcement: By 11 May 2026 Onboarding Session: 13 May 2026 Matching Phase: 15 May – 8 June 2026 Incubation Program: 8 June – 4 November 2026 Final Demo Day: November 2026 How to Apply Create an account on the official platform Complete all application sections carefully Review your responses Submit before the deadline APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Why This Opportunity Matters Many entrepreneurs have powerful ideas but lack the structure, mentorship, and network needed to grow. The EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program bridges that gap by helping founders turn purpose-driven ventures into sustainable businesses that can attract investment and deliver measurable impact. If your startup can help shape a cleaner, fairer, and more inclusive future, this is the program to apply for now. Final Reminder Applications close on 30 April 2026 . If you are ready to scale your impact and join a global ecosystem of innovators, submit your application before the deadline. title: Turn Your Agribusiness Idea Into Impact: Win Funding, Mentorship & Tools with ABF’s e-Academy 2026 Call
summary: The Agri-Business Facility for Africa , implemented by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit , has launched an exciting opportunity for aspiring agripreneurs through its Agribusiness e-Academy Call for Submissions — Window 2 (March–May 2026) . This initiative is designed to empower young entrepreneurs across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific with the knowledge, tools, and exposure needed to build investment-ready, climate-smart agribusinesses . What This Opportunity Is All About The Agribusiness e-Academy offers a free, structured learning pathway that helps participants transform raw business ideas into scalable, fundable ventures. Participants who successfully complete the program and submit their ideas stand a chance to win: €2,000 in funding Mentorship support A tablet or laptop This initiative is part of a broader effort supported by the Organisation of African Caribbean and Pacific States , the European Union, and the German Government to strengthen agricultural value chains and improve economic resilience. Why This Program Matters Launching an agribusiness is challenging—especially when it comes to securing funding. Consider this: Only 3 out of 10 early-stage businesses in Africa secure external funding Investors demand: Clear business models Strong financial viability Climate-smart strategies This pathway addresses all three— at zero cost . Who Can Apply You are eligible if you: Are 18 years or older Are based in an OACPS member state Have an agribusiness idea related to: Cocoa Maize Cashew Livestock (cattle, sheep, goats) Step-by-Step Learning Pathway The program is structured into a clear, progressive journey: 1. Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses Learn how real agribusinesses operate Understand: Cost structures Pricing strategies Revenue streams 2. Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation Identify climate risks in your value chain Learn practical: Adaptation strategies Risk management techniques 3. Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness Evaluate your business idea Test profitability Prepare to pitch to investors 4. Build Your AgriCanvas Develop your customized Business Model Canvas Turn your concept into a structured, investment-ready plan What You Can Win After completing the learning pathway and submitting your AgriCanvas: €2,000 funding Mentorship opportunities Digital tools (tablet/laptop) How to Enter Follow these steps carefully: Step 1: Register Create a free account on the atingi platform Step 2: Complete Mandatory Courses You must complete all three courses : Business Model Canvas Climate-Smart Agribusiness Investment-Ready Agribusiness Note: The third course unlocks only after completing the first two. Step 3: Prepare Your AgriCanvas Use the official template Structure your idea clearly and logically Step 4: Submit Your Idea Enter your full AgriCanvas directly into the submission form Provide: Certificate ID (mandatory) Climate-smart strategy Revenue model Capital requirements Important: No file uploads—only form entries are accepted. Submission Deadline 31 May 2026 Late or incomplete submissions (including missing certificate IDs) will not be accepted. Selection Evaluation Process Ideas are reviewed by an independent international expert panel Key evaluation criteria include: Innovation Financial viability Climate resilience Impact potential Winners will be notified via email and announced around September 2026 . About the Organisers The program is implemented by: Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit Agri-Business Facility for Africa In collaboration with: European Union (INTPA) Organisation of African Caribbean and Pacific States German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development Together, they aim to strengthen agricultural value chains , improve food security , and create jobs and income opportunities . Frequently Asked Highlights Do I need to pay anything? No. The entire program and courses are completely free . Can I submit more than one idea? No. Only one submission per applicant per window is allowed. What if I’m not selected? You can improve your idea and apply in future submission rounds. Final Thoughts This is more than a competition—it’s a launchpad for future agribusiness leaders . If you have an idea that can improve sustainability, empower communities, or transform agricultural systems, this is your opportunity to bring it to life. For more information about this opportunity and to apply, click here . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Turn Your Agribusiness Idea Into Impact: Win Funding, Mentorship Tools with ABF’s e-Academy 2026 Call appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa: Fully Funded Youth Diplomacy Simulation for African & European Leaders
summary: The AU–EU Summit Model 2026 is a prestigious youth diplomacy simulation designed to bring together young leaders from Africa and Europe to engage in high-level policy discussions, negotiations, and decision-making processes. Hosted at the African Union Commission Headquarters in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia , this unique initiative provides a powerful platform for youth to actively participate in shaping the future of Africa-Europe relations. Organized through a collaboration between Model AU–Ethiopia , the European Union Delegation to the African Union , and the African Union Commission’s Women, Gender and Youth Directorate (WGYD) , the Summit Model is a flagship youth engagement event taking place from May 12–13, 2026 . Strategically positioned between Europe Day (May 9) and Africa Day (May 25) , the event symbolizes unity, cooperation, and the central role of youth in advancing the AU–EU partnership. What is the AU–EU Summit Model? The AU–EU Summit Model is a high-impact diplomatic simulation where participants assume roles such as: Ministers Heads of State Ambassadors Commissioners Senior policy officials Through these roles, participants simulate real-world AU–EU negotiations, gaining firsthand experience in: Diplomatic dialogue Policy formulation Multilateral negotiations Leadership and governance The simulation culminates in the development of policy recommendations , which are shared with AU and EU stakeholders and representatives. Core Focus Areas of the Summit The 2026 AU–EU Summit Model will focus on key pillars of the AU–EU Partnership, including: Multilateralism Peace, Security, and Governance Green Transition and Economic Cooperation People-to-People Engagement Education and Youth Development These themes reflect the broader strategic priorities outlined in: Agenda 2063 (African Union) AU–EU Joint Vision for 2030 EU Global Gateway Strategy Why You Should Participate The AU–EU Summit Model is not just a simulation—it is a transformative leadership experience. Participants will: Engage with Global Peers Collaborate with top young leaders from Africa and Europe Gain Practical Diplomatic Experience Understand how international organizations operate Experience real-world policy-making dynamics Develop Essential Skills Public speaking Negotiation Leadership Teamwork Contribute to Policy Discussions Provide youth-driven insights into continental and global issues Deepen Knowledge of Global Frameworks AU–EU Partnership Agenda 2063 Global development strategies Program Structure and Experience The event will be held over two days (May 12–13, 2026) at the African Union Commission Headquarters . Participants will: Attend plenary sessions and committee meetings Engage in structured debates and negotiations Draft and present policy recommendations Network with fellow delegates and organizers Funding and Financial Support The organizers will provide: Program participation (fully covered) Meals during the event However, participants should note: Transportation costs are NOT covered Accommodation costs are NOT covered Personal expenses are NOT covered Participants are responsible for arranging and financing their travel and stay in Addis Ababa. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a national of an AU or EU member state Be 17–30 years old Be a: Pre-college student University student Recent graduate Be proficient in English or French Demonstrate: Passion for Agenda 2063 Interest in the AU–EU Partnership Commitment to global development and diplomacy Identify as a globally minded citizen committed to positive change Application Process To apply for the AU–EU Summit Model 2026: Complete the official Google Form registration Submit your application before the deadline Await confirmation after the application review process Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all details are accurate. Application Deadline Deadline: April 30, 2026 Time: 5:00 p.m. (Ethiopian Time) Late applications will not be considered. Why This Opportunity Stands Out The AU–EU Summit Model 2026 is a standout opportunity because it: Provides direct exposure to AU and EU systems Empowers youth to influence real policy discussions Builds future diplomatic leaders Strengthens Africa-Europe collaboration Offers a prestigious platform for global engagement Click Here to learn more about the AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa. Discover more global Conference opportunities on OFY Here. title: ElevenLabs Ambassador Program 2026: How to Join, Benefits, Eligibility, and Opportunities for Creators and Community Builders
summary: The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is a unique global opportunity designed for creators, innovators, and community leaders who are passionate about shaping the future of AI-powered voice technology. If you are already using ElevenLabs or interested in advancing your influence in the AI and content creation space, this program offers a powerful platform to grow your impact while gaining exclusive access to tools, funding, and opportunities. ElevenLabs, a leading company in AI voice generation, is building a global network of ambassadors to help showcase what is possible with its cutting-edge technology. Through this program, selected individuals contribute by hosting events, creating content, and engaging communities around AI audio innovation. What is the ElevenLabs Ambassador Program? The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is a global initiative that allows individuals to actively promote and expand the use of ElevenLabs tools within their communities or digital platforms. It is structured around two primary participation tracks: Event Hosting and Content Creation. Participants in the program act as representatives of ElevenLabs, helping to educate others, demonstrate real-world use cases, and contribute feedback that shapes the development of the platform. Ambassador Tracks: Choose Your Path Event Host Track As an Event Host, you will focus on building and engaging a local community. This includes organizing activities such as meetups, hackathons, workshops, demo nights, and listening sessions. ElevenLabs supports Event Hosts by providing funding, branding materials, and logistical resources. This track is ideal for individuals who enjoy leadership, networking, and community engagement. Content Creator Track Content Creators focus on producing educational and engaging materials that demonstrate how to use ElevenLabs tools. This includes tutorials, product demos, workflow breakdowns, and reviews. Content can be shared across platforms such as YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, LinkedIn, or other digital channels. This track is perfect for influencers, educators, and digital creators. Applicants can choose one track or apply for both, depending on their interests and capabilities. Benefits of Joining the ElevenLabs Ambassador Program Participants gain access to a wide range of benefits designed to support growth and innovation: Platform credits to use ElevenLabs tools Early access to new features and product updates Funding and resources for hosting events Performance-based compensation for content creators Direct access to the ElevenLabs team Exclusive merchandise and branding materials Invitations to special events and networking opportunities Although the program is primarily community-driven and not a traditional salaried role, content creators can unlock paid opportunities depending on performance and audience engagement. How to Contribute as an Ambassador For Event Hosts: Organize and manage local events Represent ElevenLabs within their communities Provide feedback to improve the platform For Content Creators: Develop high-quality tutorials and guides Share practical workflows and insights Demonstrate use cases and product features Application Process Submit an application detailing your intended contributions Participate in a discovery phase if shortlisted Attend a brief introductory call with the ElevenLabs team Receive onboarding resources and begin contributing Successful applicants will receive a welcome package, including onboarding guides, access to community platforms, and initial resources to get started. Eligibility Criteria Must be at least 18 years old Must have an active ElevenLabs account Demonstrate prior use of the platform Have an existing audience, community, or clear contribution plan Deadline There is currently no fixed application deadline. Applications are open on a rolling basis, but interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as selection may be competitive. Why You Should Apply The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is more than just an opportunity; it is a gateway into the rapidly growing field of AI-driven audio technology. Whether you are a student, creator, entrepreneur, or tech enthusiast, this program enables you to build authority in AI, expand your global network, gain hands-on experience, and contribute to shaping the future of voice technology. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here.
